title: 24:39 ГўВҖВ” October 16, 2024
body_text:
24:39 ГўВҖВ” October 16, 2024
Update to the Interac e-Transfer Online Administration System (OAS) Part
We updated the
Interac
e-Transfer
ГӮВ®
Online Administration System (OAS) Part as follows:
Electronic Transactions Volume
Interac
e
ГўВҖВ‘
Transfer Online Administration System (OAS) Part
Section 1.1, Overview of the
Interac
eГўВҖВ‘Transfer Online Administration System (OAS)
We added a note describing how financial institutions using the OAS Fraud Analyst role can use OAS to manage fraud suspicious
Interac
e-Transfer transactions.
Section 1.2, Contacts, Websites, and Other Resources
We amended the contact description for the Central 1 Fraud Analyst System (FAS) team with information relating to the OAS Fraud Analyst role.
Section 2.1, Definitions
We amended the definition of a
Blocked List
to make it clear that transfers can still be sent through
Interac
e-Transfer when an email address or mobile phone number involved on the transfer is on a blocked list, although the transfer will be under
InteracГўВҖВҷs
scrutiny.
We amended the definition of a
Contact
to include a banking account number as a way of knowing a recipient (for Account Number Routing).
We amended the definition of
Disabled/Enabled
to mention that an OAS Fraud Analyst can enable or disable a profile.
We amended the definition of
Failed
to clarify that only outgoing transfers are subject to limit violations that could fail the transaction.
We amended the definition of
Fulfill Request
to explain that a request and the fulfilling transfer are cross-referenced against each other in their transaction details in OAS.
We added definitions for
Funds Recovery Request
,
Handle
, and
OAS Fraud Analyst
.
Section 2.2, Frequently Asked Questions
We amended any answers containing instructions requesting FAS to perform any action, with instructions to perform the action through an OAS Fraud Analyst.
We added a new table of FAQs relating to the OAS Fraud Analyst Role.
Section 3.1, Overview of OAS Access and Administration
We added a description of the OAS Fraud Analyst user type.
Section 3.5, Logging in to OAS
We added a description of the landing page seen by an OAS Fraud Analyst at login.
Section 3.8, Managing OAS User Accounts ГўВҖВ“ OAS Fraud Analysts
New section
Chapter 5, Viewing a CustomerГўВҖВҷs
Interac
eГўВҖВ‘Transfer Profile on OAS
We changed any references to secure site with references to Client Centre.
Chapter 6, The OAS Fraud Analyst Role
New chapter
Chapter 8, Troubleshooting Tracing Requests
We updated the description of interrupted transfers as a potential reason for failed transfers.
Questions
Client Support Services, phone
1-888-889-7878
, Option 1, email
support@central1.com
.
